title: dubautia laxa
intro: Dubautia laxa, also known as Hawaiian Tree Daisy, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, characterized by its white to pink flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dubautia laxa include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling, as well as prevent infections. Dubautia laxa is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to promote overall well-being.
